```json
  "findings": [
    "Joist hangers are fastened with roofing nails instead of the manufacturer-specified joist hanger nails or structural screws.",
    "Roofing nails have smaller heads and smooth shanks, visible by the small, round, often dimpled head pattern rather than the larger flat head of joist hanger nails.",
    "Wrong fastener type in joist hangers creates inadequate shear and withdrawal resistance at the hanger-to-beam/ledger connection.",
    "This is a classic, easily identified defect by head size and pattern."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove all roofing nails from the joist hangers.",
    "Install Simpson Strong-Tie or manufacturer-specified joist hanger nails (e.g., Teco, HDG common nails of correct length/diameter) or approved structural screws at every hole in the hanger.",
    "Verify fastener head type, length, and diameter match the hanger manufacturer's installation instructions.",
    "Re-inspect all hangers on the job for similar improper fasteners."
  ],
  "rationale": "Joist hangers are a critical structural connector. Manufacturer instructions and the IRC require the fasteners specified by the hanger manufacturer. Roofing nails do not provide the required shear, withdrawal, or bearing capacity, creating a high-risk structural defect. The small head size and pattern of roofing nails is a clear visual cue of non-compliance.",
  "references": [
    "IRC R507.2.1 / R507.5 - deck/joist hanger fastening per manufacturer instructions",
    "Simpson Strong-Tie catalog - joist hanger installation requires specified hanger nails or approved fasteners"
  ]
}
```
